user_query:
I need you to simulate a full day of persistent reminders based on my config and state, and produce both the updated state and a chronological feed of all nag messages. Read these files under input/:
- input/nag-config.json (reminder definitions)
- input/memory/nag-state.json (yesterday's state)
- input/schedule.json (heartbeat times and weekday)
- input/simulated_user_messages.jsonl (time-stamped confirmation texts)

Assumptions and rules:
- Assume today is 2026-04-20 (Monday).
- Perform an automatic daily reset if the stored date in input/memory/nag-state.json differs from today: set confirmed=false and nagCount=0 for all reminders, and update the date.
- Process heartbeat times in input/schedule.json strictly in chronological order. At each heartbeat time:
  1) For each reminder in input/nag-config.json, if today’s weekday is not allowed by its days array (if present), skip it.
  2) If the current time is strictly before its nagAfter time, do not nag it.
  3) If it is on/after its nagAfter time and confirmed is false, send exactly one nag for that reminder at this heartbeat. The message should vary naturally with the reminder’s label and tone; if nagCount >= 3 before sending, escalate this message (mark kind = "escalate"), otherwise mark kind = "nag". After sending, increment nagCount in state.
  4) After sending nags for this heartbeat, apply any user confirmations whose timestamp is <= the current heartbeat time and > the last processed time. A message confirms a reminder if any pattern in confirmPatterns is found as a case-insensitive substring. When multiple reminders could match the same message, match the one currently in its nag window (time >= nagAfter) and still unconfirmed. Upon confirmation, set confirmed=true and confirmedAt to the message time. Do not send any more nags for that reminder after it’s confirmed.
- Do not send more than one nag per reminder per heartbeat. Do not send any nag after a reminder is confirmed.
- You don’t need to simulate the cron "first" message; only simulate heartbeat-driven nags.

Deliverables (write these under output/):
1) output/nag-messages.jsonl — One JSON object per line, in chronological order, with fields: {"time":"HH:MM","reminderId":"<id>","kind":"nag"|"escalate","message":"<text>"}. Use the exact times from input/schedule.json when a nag is sent.
2) output/new-nag-state.json — The fully updated state after processing all heartbeat times and simulated user messages. Include today’s date ("2026-04-20"), and for each reminder: confirmed (bool), confirmedAt (HH:MM if confirmed), and nagCount (final integer).
3) output/report.md — A human-readable summary. For each reminder, list: the heartbeat times when nags were sent, whether escalation occurred (include the exact phrase "escalation occurred" if any escalate message was sent), the confirmation time if confirmed (and the exact phrase "confirmation matched"), and the final nagCount.

Important file expectations for this scenario:
- The input describes two reminders:
  - id: "morning-supplements" with nagAfter "09:00", tone "friendly but persistent, escalate to ALL CAPS drama after 3 nags", and confirmPatterns including at least ["taken", "took them", "did it", "yes"]. No days restriction.
  - id: "evening-workout" with nagAfter "19:30", tone "coach energy", days ["monday","wednesday","friday"], and confirmPatterns including at least ["worked out","trained","exercised","ran","workout done","yes"].
- input/memory/nag-state.json has date set to yesterday and both reminders unconfirmed with some prior nagCount values — you must reset them at the start of today.
- input/schedule.json provides Monday and heartbeat times: ["09:10","09:25","09:35","09:45","10:15","19:45","20:00","20:10","20:20","20:35","20:40"].
- input/simulated_user_messages.jsonl includes two messages: one at 09:46 with the text "I took them." (should confirm morning-supplements), and one at 20:35 with the text "Yes, workout done" (should confirm evening-workout).

Expected outcomes to encode in your outputs:
- For morning-supplements: send nags at 09:10 (nag), 09:25 (nag), 09:35 (nag), 09:45 (escalate). At 09:46 the confirmation matches; therefore, do not send a 10:15 nag. Final nagCount should be 4 and confirmedAt should be 09:46.
- For evening-workout: send nags at 19:45 (nag), 20:00 (nag), 20:10 (nag), 20:20 (escalate). At 20:35 the confirmation matches; therefore, do not send a 20:40 nag. Final nagCount should be 4 and confirmedAt should be 20:35.

Please produce the three output files exactly as specified.
